text.text:
★★★★★

Beyla is one of the most surprising vegan experiences I’ve had in Copenhagen. The cuisine is refined yet deeply authentic: dishes crafted with great attention to detail, intense and perfectly balanced flavors, and a strong focus on high-quality ingredients.

The atmosphere is warm, intimate, and welcoming, and the service is attentive without ever being intrusive. You can clearly sense a strong, coherent vision, both in the culinary offering and in the restaurant’s identity.

A place that truly shows how vegan cuisine can be elegant, contemporary, and deeply satisfying. Highly recommended.

text.text:
We came to Beyla in early September as it was just a short walk from where we were staying; a little tucked away, which made it even more fun to find! We loved the gorgeous space inside- It is cosy, bright, and happy and there’s even a speakeasy- tyle toilet that you have to check out!

The menu is completely plant based and designed to share. You can go for the Beyla tasting menu for 399 DKK (around $94–95 AUD), or choose à la carte like we did. They also offer lovely wine pairings and a great selection of natural wines by the glass. The staff were warm, friendly, and guided us through what dishes were popular.

Everything we ate was so fresh, colourful, and delicious. Some of our highlights were the Beyla Fried Mushrooms, Bourbon and Smoked Cheese Slider, Risotto of the day, and if they have them in season,  the tomatoes!

Beyla is part of The Ark Collection, created by a group of passionate people who also run Restaurant Ark and Bistro Lupa. Their goal is to show how exciting and satisfying plant based food can be ; not as a trend, but as a way to eat that’s thoughtful, sustainable, and full of creativity. They even work closely with Denmark’s only organic urban mushroom farm, which they co-own, to source incredible produce. You can really taste the care that goes into each dish.

I highly recommend visiting Beyla when you’re in Copenhagen — especially if you love beautiful, vibrant plant based food and friendly service in a space that just makes you feel happy.

text.text:
I went to Alexander’s Bistro using the Early Bird offer – three courses for around 260 kroner – and it was honestly a really good deal. The starter was a souæ, and it was absolutely delicious. Definitely a 5/5. The flavour was deep and rich.

For the main course we had confit de canard. It was fine, but the duck itself could have had more flavour, and the fries felt a bit out of place for this kind of dish. Also they were kind of dry. We did get a few different sauces and dips, which helped make up for the lack of seasoning in the duck.

For dessert we got Peter Beier ice cream, which is always nice.

One thing that annoyed me a lot was that I had to pay same for cocktail without alcohol as one with alcohol. 135 kr for a drink with passion fruit sirup and no alkohol is insane. They didn't put the non alcohol prices on the menu but I assumed that it would be cheaper (because thats how it usually is).

Overall, it’s a nice restaurant with a cosy atmosphere and a great location. The Early Bird offer was genuinely good value for money, but I’m not sure I’d come back again, simply because the food didn’t feel memorable enough (except for the soup).

text.text:
An Unexpected Evening at Aamanns Genbo

We had high expectations when we pre-ordered a vegetarian tasting menu at Aamanns Genbo. Unfortunately, the food did not live up to those expectations.

The starter, a croquette, had a strange doughy texture that left us a bit puzzled. The main course, consisting of tartlets with salad, was unfortunately heavy with a wet, soggy crust. The entire main course was smothered in grated cheese and so salty that some components almost burned in the mouth.

The dessert was okay, but unfortunately, not enough to elevate the meal.

On the positive side, the service was top-notch. The staff was incredibly friendly and attentive, which certainly lifted our experience. As an additional gesture, they gave us a free cafe latte as compensation for the food.

All in all, Aamanns Genbo is not a place I can recommend for the food. However, if you’re looking for a drink and good service, it might be worth a visit.

text.text:
We went to Dolores with two tipster coupons for the 8 course dinner menu. Overall, it was a nice evening, but the food experience itself was somewhat underwhelming.
There was definitely some good dishes, like the eggplant with black garlic and harissa and the saffron arancini, but the rest was quite under seasoned, under salted or simple to the point that i could easily have  made it myself at home (like the tomato salad, flat bread and potatoes). Oddly enough, the fries however were so salty we had to scrape off clumped up salt from them.

I noticed that a lot of the dishes they highlight on their front page of their website, were not included in the set menu, so if I were to come back, I would definitely try a la carte, as maybe thats where the good stuff is hiding!

While the staff was nice and we were welcomed kindly, we did stoop over one particular oddity. When ordering our wine, we were not offered to taste it first, which is something we personally have never experienced in a sit down restaurant of this price point. Instead, we were poured a (generous, I will say) glass right away. I didn’t like my wine very much - and fair enough, that’s on me - but usually this is something that restaurants would prefer avoiding by simply offering a quick taste first.

The decorating and ambiance of the place is nice and charming, and I did notice that a lot of guests seemed to know someone working in the restaurant, which did add a nice atmosphere. If I were the kind of person to love having lots of conversation with the staff, though, I might have felt a bit overlooked, since the attention was mainly on the guests that the staff knew. Personally, I didn’t mind.

Our biggest caveat however is, that for a place that is focused on ‘mission district’ food, we expected to see Mexican inspired dishes as Mission District is particularly home to a huge Mexican-American community. This wasn’t the case.
Seems like Dolores is still trying to settle in, and unfortunately, that resulted in a pretty ambiguous experience.

I wouldn’t flat out tell people to steer clear of this place, as I’m sure many others would enjoy it a lot. The menu was well priced with the tipster coupon, but I wouldn’t get it again for the full price.

Unfortunately, this place just didn’t live up to our expectations.

We went to Dolores with two tipster coupons for the 8 course dinner menu. Overall, it was a nice evening, but the food experience itself was somewhat underwhelming.
There was definitely some good dishes, like the eggplant with black garlic and harissa and the saffron arancini, but the rest was quite under seasoned, under salted or simple to the point that i could easily have  made it myself at home (like the tomato salad, flat bread and potatoes). Oddly enough, the fries however were so salty we had to scrape off clumped up salt from them.

I noticed that a lot of the dishes they highlight on their front page of their website, were not included in the set menu, so if I were to come back, I would definitely try a la carte, as maybe thats where the good stuff is hiding!

While the staff was nice and we were welcomed kindly, we did stoop over one particular oddity. When ordering our wine, we were not offered to taste it first, which is something we personally have never experienced in a sit down restaurant of this price point. Instead, we were poured a (generous, I will say) glass right away. I didn’t like my wine very much - and fair enough, that’s on me - but usually this is something that restaurants would prefer avoiding by simply offering a quick taste first.

The decorating and ambiance of the place is nice and charming, and I did notice that a lot of guests seemed to know someone working in the restaurant, which did add a nice atmosphere. If I were the kind of person to love having lots of conversation with the staff, though, I might have felt a bit overlooked, since the attention was mainly on the guests that the staff knew. Personally, I didn’t mind.

Our biggest caveat however is, that for a place that is focused on ‘mission district’ food, we expected to see Mexican inspired dishes as Mission District is particularly home to a huge Mexican-American community. This wasn’t the case.
Seems like Dolores is still trying to settle in, and unfortunately, that resulted in a pretty ambiguous experience.

I wouldn’t flat out tell people to steer clear of this place, as I’m sure many others would enjoy it a lot. The menu was well priced with the tipster coupon, but I wouldn’t get it again for the full price.

Unfortunately, this place just didn’t live up to our expectations.

We tried the 7 course tasting menu through Tipster. I must say that the staff were exceptional, really explaining each dish, the ingredients and which region of Italy it is influenced by. They were also very attentive 😊.
The food was a bit lacking, specifically the charcuterie could’ve used some cheese options, veggie main was only roasted veggies, and no tea was available on the drink menu, only coffee.
The highlights were definitely the delicious Italian oil served with the warm bread, and the dessert.
Overall we enjoyed our time in the restaurant but don’t think we will come back.

My wife and I discovered the restaurant via Tipster and purchase of voucher for the degustation menu. It was a very good experience from the start to the end.
Welcoming, professional and professional staff providing good advices and information on the dishes and wines (extensive italian wine list).
The menu was excellent, the dishes were tasty, made with care and fresh ingredients. Good portions and variety.
The wines matched perfectly the dishes.
The dishes were served at perfect intervals. the service is very efficient service; nothing is rushed.
Comfy and quiet atmosphere allowing to relax, enjoy the food and conversation with your companions.
The restaurant is located in a quiet area of new Carlsberg neighbourhood with a nice terrasse that will be very pleasant to sit for lunch.
We highly recommend the restaurant and will gladly go back in the future. Most likely our new go to Italian restaurant in Copenhagen.

text.text:
For an unrivalled chain burger experience, this an unmissable spot. The quality of the butter burger beef, sauce and salty fries is incomparable to anything in the UK. The crispy burger bits sewn through the patty, and the drenching in salt adds so much flavour to a simple meal.

The options for sauces were significant, about 8 to choose from, with a seasonal sauce (here being truffle mayo) changes frequently. Service was incredibly easy and friendly. Our order was taken and burgers delivered pronto. Since the open kitchen and seating arrangement is all exposed you can pleasantly enjoy watching your food get fried up and served with a smile.

For the quality of burger, speed of service and lovely atmosphere, this is without a doubt the best chain restaurant I’ve been to in my life. Absolutely recommend cheers :)

text.text:
After a beautiful beginning to our day at Home of Carlsberg - with an exhibition and a guided walk around the place, I had arranged for my parents and my girlfriend and I to enjoy some lovely italian food at the Tramonto (Sunset) Rooftop restaurant. And we were not dissapointed!

Absolutely lovely service and a beautiful view 😊 I would even recommend - if one needs a run to the restrooms - to stop by the big Windows by the front of the bar to really see the skyline and the Chimney of Carl Jacobsens Ny Carlsberg.

The vibe was really nice, and we loved the open kitchen which allowed us to follow the process of the food being cooked. Also, our waiter was an absolute treat, and made my girlfriend (who was raise in Rome) feel like she was almost home again.

The food was lovely - but overpriced. This is the only downside. Although pricey,  the flavours were well-balanced and the the pasta perfectly al dente. The cheese board had a lovely variety, with some very good flavours as well, that was complimented by the condiments.

The atmosphere is warm and perfect for a relaxed evening. The food was full of flavor, and the prices are very reasonable for the good quality you get.

A special mention goes to our waiter, Gabriel, who was extremely polite, respectful, and attentive throughout the tasting menu. His service truly elevated the experience.

The only small note is about the dessert: all the elements were very tasty and well balanced in flavor, but the ice cream had a slightly grainy texture — perhaps due to the stabilizers not being properly incorporated during preparation.

While it wasn’t unpleasant, it didn’t quite match the smoothness and quality of the other courses.. Still, it was a sweet finish to a beautiful meal.

Overall, a very positive experience. We would definitely come back.

Unassuming bakery offering traditional danish pastries and sourdough bread. Loved the deeply flavoured sweet and savoury yeast dough bun with coffee glaze. The pastries with croissant doughs were also fab - the tebirkes with poppy seeds and the Kringle with prunes, cardamon, brandy - beautifully laminated, crispy and flaky, soft inside. The cardamon bun was probably my favourite from this trip.
All buns were sweeter than what I’m used to but flavours and textures work really well.
Great staff too!

text.text:
For such a restaurant, the service was not good enough. We went during Dining Week and I got the impression that, since it was cheaper to go during this week, there was less interest in providing the same level of service as to other guests.

After asking for a wine recommendation, I was only given the name of one wine with no description, even after asking if it would go with the menu or if there were any other options. The wine was also not the best match for the menu. The menu was mostly boring with no real surprises.

The food was probably really good quality, so I have no complaints in that respect. However, it just felt too standard for the price we were paying. The dessert was the only thing that stood out, with a strong sourness from the blueberries, an earthy flavour from the beetroot, and a fresh cream cheese ice cream. The ambience was lovely and the restaurant looked amazing.

Still, the menu did not convince me.

Fantastic restaurant with lovely cocktails and great service.

We ordered a bit of everything to share and brought it back to our apartment - which was absolutely the right decision as everything we ordered was fantastic - and most was not too spicy just at a perfect level (although this will depend on your tolerance)

The churro was also great.

The highlight for me was the fish tacos - which were perhaps the best tacos I’ve ever had…. We’re perfect in every way from fish cooking to sauce ratio to veg to tortilla just a masterpiece really..

We had a margarita as we waited for the food to be ready which was very well made (and pretty strong!)

Strongly recommend to any in the area.

We ordered a bit of everything to share and brought it back to our apartment - which was absolutely the right decision as everything we ordered was fantastic - and most was not too spicy just at a perfect level (although this will depend on your tolerance)

The churro was also great.

The highlight for me was the fish tacos - which were perhaps the best tacos I’ve ever had…. We’re perfect in every way from fish cooking to sauce ratio to veg to tortilla just a masterpiece really..

We had a margarita as we waited for the food to be ready which was very well made (and pretty strong!)

Strongly recommend to any in the area.

text.text: I was looking for something to eat quite late in the evening. This developing area is very quiet and low on gastronomical choice at the moment. I was very fortunate enough to catch these pizza making lads before they closed for the night. The pizza I ate last night was absolutely delicious. Great, tasty toppings and the crust was the right amount of crunchy/crispy without being over baked. I’m from Melbourne, Australia, where great pizzas are pretty easy to find. I live now in Oslo, Norway, where it’s not easy at all. So here in Copenhagen I was very happy to see that I need not have worried about Scandi consistency. Top marks to the chefs! This neighbourhood is very lucky!

text.text:
Surt is easily one of my favorite pizza spots in Copenhagen. Located in the heart of the historic Carlsberg district (right by the elephants), it offers a world-class experience that manages to be both cozy and incredibly high-quality.

The Magic of the Dough
The founder, Giuseppe Oliva (who helped start Bæst), is a true master. The name "Surt" means "sour" in Danish, referring to their 100% sourdough process with no industrial yeast. The result is a crust that is light, perfectly charred, and much easier to digest than your typical pizza. It’s exactly as good as the hype suggests.

The Ingredients & Authenticity
What makes Surt stand out is the blend of local organic produce and premium Italian imports.
•	The Hindsholm Pizza: My absolute favorite. The pork sausage from the Hindsholm pigs is incredible, especially with the fermented chili. I’ve tried several others across many visits, and the quality never slips.
•	Authentic Vibe: You can really feel the Italian roots here. Using ingredients sourced directly from Italy makes the flavors feel truly authentic, yet it fits perfectly into the modern, industrial vibe of Carlsberg Byen.

A Couple of "Local" Suggestions
•	The Sharing Factor: I love the concept, but I wish they wouldn't "force" the sharing experience quite so much. Sometimes you just want all the pizzas brought to the table at the same time unless asked otherwise!
•	Lunch Hours: Honestly, the only other negative is that they aren't open for lunch, why keep this goodness just for the evening? (Haha, but seriously!)

text.text:
SURT Carlsberg serves the best Italian pizza I have ever tasted. The crust was perfectly baked—crispy on the outside, soft inside—and the flavors felt fresh and balanced. The toppings were not overloaded, but every bite was full of taste.

I am used to pizzas often being very heavy with cheese and spices, but here the style was more authentic and natural. I really enjoyed this difference. It felt lighter yet more satisfying.

We tried all 7 pizzas on the menu, and each one was delicious. My personal favorite was the Shrooms pizza – rich, earthy, and full of flavor. Drinks were excellent too, including Negroni, Gin & Tonic, and a lovely Red Wine. For dessert, we had Tiramisu and Chocolate Mousse, both a perfect sweet ending to the meal.

The place has a modern and cozy atmosphere, and the service was warm. For me, it was not just a meal, but an experience of true Italian pizza in Denmark. Highly recommended!

text.text:
While it does sound ridiculous to pay over 200dkk for a pizza, damn is it worth it. It is probably the best pizza I’ve had in Copenhagen, and you’ll surely not find something similar in texture and quality of ingredients at a lower price. So crispy on the outside and soft of the inside. We also tried the Focaccia with Olive oil and Tiramisu as a desert and all that was very tasty too!
The one improvement could be the portion size of the glass of wine. I would rather pay 15, 20dkk extra per glass but at least have a proper glass of wine rather than just a sip. The wine tasted lovely at least.

The best pizza in the world! ⭐️ Every time we go to Copenhagen, our first program is to visiting Surt, because it's a one of a kind experience.

They make the best sourdough pizza, what we have ever tasted in their own wood fired oven. You can see every step of the preparation from your table and the flames are mesmerizing! 🔥

Trying out the famous Hindsholm pizza is definitely a must, but do not skip the other dishes as well, because they are not just tasty and look beautiful, but you will feel the flavour of Sicily in every single bite. Great wines and great cocktails too. 🍷

Book in advance and have a very special night thanks to the amazing staff there. 🙏

Wow, had the 5 course dinner at Alexander’s Bistro and this was extraordinary delicious. We paid 495 for a wonderful 5 course menu that was  inspired by the season. Every mouthful was full of taste and inspiration and with a context and flavor you could not have made yourself. This is Nordic seasonal kitchen at its best. This is true value for money and you get a full stomach. The coffee we had in the “Next room” in front of the the fireplace with board games. This place is a gem and highly recommended. Also you can have a coffee or cocktail in the sun outside and enjoy the vibrant living of #carlsbergbyen.
This place gets my warm recommendation and we will be back ❤️

highlightedText.text: "We were super excited to try Surt when planning our trip to Copenhagen."

text.text: We were super excited to try Surt when planning our trip to Copenhagen. We travel frequently, and often like to try the best pizza in whatever cities we're in. We ordered focaccia, a salad, and 2 Margarita pizzas. We had 2 vegetarians in the group, so our options were a little more limited. The flavor of crust, sauce, and of course buffalo mozzarella were so delicious. Phenomenal, in fact. So excited to go back!! Thank you for a wonderful experience!
